intro: The ransomware landscape from April 12–19, 2026, was dominated by the threat actor Payouts King, alongside Interlock and Rhysida. This archive of 19 articles details their activity, which heavily targeted the healthcare, logistics, and automotive sectors, with significant incidents also affecting data-analytics and entertainment. Geographically, operations impacted Europe, Turkey, the Netherlands, and North America, with several campaigns having a global reach. Technical analysis frequently centered on the exploitation of CVE-2026-34197, a vulnerability with a CVSS score of 8.8. Of the incidents covered, the severity was overwhelmingly high, with 15 articles classified as such and one as critical.
